objective: Review-only audit of Depth Guard, leaf-only worker rules, bounded spawning, and anti-recursion protections.
summary_findings: Depth Guard is well documented: commander and lint artifacts consistently state 50 squad leads, 5 workers each, premium-model policy, and mandatory worker leaf-lock.

Primary gaps are runtime enforcement gaps rather than documentation gaps: worker generation/can_launch are not used as hard recursion guards, squad-lead anti-recursion is not explicitly locked like worker prompts, and success checks are mostly post-hoc proof checks.
Stop/recovery telemetry is incomplete: launcher teardown updates top-level state only, not commander child ledgers/state, so bounded-spawn failure handling is weaker during teardown/recovery.
SKILL.md and lint coverage reinforce the policy, but lint remains string-based and does not verify launcher gating or nested-recursion prevention.
consensus_notes: Consensus: policy/instruction coverage is strong across agent docs, skill docs, and lint.
Consensus: runtime safeguards are weaker than the written policy, especially for recursion prevention and teardown telemetry.
conflict_notes: No material conflicts across worker findings; lint coverage findings complement, rather than contradict, runtime enforcement gaps.
